mechanism_narrative: A higher and rising FEDFUNDS level tightens discount rates and financing conditions, reducing the present value of equity cash flows and predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 3.0 percent in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month or 6-month changes in FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A downside crossing in retail sales momentum signals weakening household demand and lower expected corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 343623 million dollars in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS downside momentum crossings have non-negative average 20-day S&P 500 forward returns or hit-rate below baseline in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion combined with a falling unemployment rate signals broad labor-income growth without visible labor-market stress, supporting consumption and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 133347 thousand and UNRATE was 5.1 percent in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE does not deliver higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than either single-series condition alone.

mechanism_narrative: Very high housing starts during a tightening cycle indicate rate-sensitive demand vulnerable to financing-cost pressure, leading to weaker equity returns as housing activity mean-reverts.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above 2000 and FEDFUNDS 6-month change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 2038 thousand units and FEDFUNDS was 3.0 percent in the latest available snapshot
regime_hint: late-cycle housing boom with monetary tightening
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with HOUST above 2000 and rising FEDFUNDS have positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or no subsequent HOUST deceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects improving real output and operating leverage for listed firms, so higher INDPRO momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 118.641 in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if 6-month INDPRO momentum has Rank IC below 0.03 with 60-day S&P 500 returns in any 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation pressure paired with a rising policy rate signals a central-bank reaction function that tightens financial conditions, compresses valuation multiples, and predicts negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change > 3 percent and FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 194.1 and FEDFUNDS was 3.0 percent in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-inflation and rising-FEDFUNDS condition is not followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than periods with high CPIAUCSL momentum alone.

mechanism_narrative: An upward crossing in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and rising recession risk, triggering risk-off repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change crosses above +0.3 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 5.1 percent in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE increases greater than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth accompanied by firm retail sales identifies a broad demand expansion that supports earnings expectations and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 was 11088.8 billion chained 2017 dollars and RSAFS was 343623 million dollars in the latest snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous positive GDPC1 and RSAFS momentum fails to produce higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than unconditional returns in walk-forward testing.
